card_description: Noos is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Noos. Context: "Dick analyzes how our sense of causality is manipulated by a higher intelligence (Noos)."

linked_segments.summary: Dick analyzes how our sense of causality is manipulated by a higher intelligence (Noos). He suggests that what we perceive as "cause and effect" is often a concealment used by Zebra to move the world
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_114.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick outlines a three-stage progression of the human experience with the Divine: 1) Suffering/Dread (The world is real and hostile), 2) Amazement/Pantheism (The world is alive and animate), and 3) Bli
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_128.txt
